name: Parque Nacional de Guadarrama – circular to Puerto de la Morcuera desde Rascafría
name_original: Parque Nacional de Guadarrama – circular al Puerto de la Morcuera desde Rascafría
region_intro: Touring cycling around Rascafría explores the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Guadarrama, featuring significant elevation changes and scenic mountain passes. The region is characterized by forested valleys, open landscapes, and views of the Pinilla Reservoir. Rascafría bike trail distances and elevation vary, offering options from challenging climbs to more accessible routes along riverbeds. Best touring cycling routes around Rascafría The most popular touring cycling route is Sierra de Guadarrama Three Passes Loop, a 55.2 miles (88.9 km) trail that takes 6 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates multiple mountain passes within the Sierra de Guadarrama. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Parque Nacional de Guadarrama – circular to Puerto de la Morcuera desde Rascafría, a difficult 19.7 miles (31.8 km) path. This route offers panoramic viewpoints within the national park, including the Puerto de la Morcuera. Local touring cyclists also love the El Paular Monastery – Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint loop from Rascafría, a 17.2 miles (27.6 km) trail leading through river valleys and past historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour 48 minutes. Touring cycling around Rascafría is defined by mountain passes, forested valleys, and reservoir viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family friendly bike routes to advanced touring bike routes. The routes in Rascafría are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 3000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Rascafría's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Rascafría?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;16 touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Rascafría, offering a variety of experiences from easy river valley rides to challenging mountain pass ascents within the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Rascafría?
A: Yes, Rascafría offers &lt;strong&gt;4 easy touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt;. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14889718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Paular Monastery – Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint loop from Rascafría&lt;/a&gt;, which is 27.6 km long and leads through river valleys and past historical sites, suitable for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are the most challenging touring cycling routes in Rascafría?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Rascafría has &lt;strong&gt;9 difficult routes&lt;/strong&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31588313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cotos Pass – Monastery of El Paular loop from Rascafría&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 102.7 km route with significant elevation changes, navigating multiple mountain passes in the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Rascafría?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Rascafría are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959753114/parque-nacional-de-guadarrama-circular-to-puerto-de-la-morcuera-desde-rascafria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de Guadarrama – circular to Puerto de la Morcuera desde Rascafría&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic viewpoints within the national park.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring cycling routes around Rascafría?
A: Touring cycling in Rascafría offers diverse scenery, including forested valleys, open landscapes, and views of the Pinilla Reservoir. You'll also encounter impressive mountain passes within the Sierra de Guadarrama, such as those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190686554/sierra-de-guadarrama-three-passes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Guadarrama Three Passes Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see along the routes?
A: Along the touring cycling routes, you can explore various natural and historical landmarks. Highlights include the historic El Paular Monastery, the scenic Pinilla Reservoir, and natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3692534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalara Glacial Cirque&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2980144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charca Verde&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by mountain features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Rascafría?
A: The touring cycling routes in Rascafría are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.4 stars from over 90 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the stunning mountain scenery, and the well-maintained paths through the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Is Rascafría suitable for touring cyclists who enjoy significant elevation changes?
A: Absolutely. Rascafría is situated in the mountainous Sierra de Guadarrama, meaning many routes feature significant elevation changes and challenging mountain passes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14886116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morcuera Pass loop from Rascafría&lt;/a&gt; are perfect for those who enjoy climbing.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent views of local reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14889718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Paular Monastery – Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint loop from Rascafría&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights the Pinilla Reservoir, offering picturesque waterside cycling.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Rascafría?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Rascafría is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is typically milder and more stable, making mountain passes more accessible and enjoyable. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out, especially for higher elevations.

Q: Are there opportunities to combine cycling with exploring natural parks?
A: Yes, Rascafría is located near the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, and many touring cycling routes traverse or offer views into the park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959753114/parque-nacional-de-guadarrama-circular-to-puerto-de-la-morcuera-desde-rascafria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de Guadarrama – circular to Puerto de la Morcuera desde Rascafría&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing an immersive experience within the national park's stunning landscapes.
tips:
It is located in the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range, between the municipalities of Miraflores de la Sierra and Rascafría, in the Community of Madrid.

From Miraflores de la Sierra (1200m), the route ascends approximately 600m over 10km (average gradient 4–6%), and after reaching the summit, it descends 13km to Rascafría (1150m) with a gradient of 4.5–7%.

Las Presillas are natural pools located in Rascafria, in the El Paular Valley.
They are included in a recreational area that has wide green meadows and a bathing area (divided into three natural pools) in the Lozoya riverbed, with spectacular views of Pico Peñalara.

The history of this monastery can be grouped around three main axes: El Paular-Cartuja (1390-1835), El Paular disentailed (1835-1954) and El Paular recovered for monastic life (1954-until today).
huerta_paularThe foundation of the Charterhouse of Santa María de El Paular dates back to 1390. The House of Trastamara granted it great privileges and defrayed the costs of the works throughout the reign. Juan I began to erect the first Cartuja de Castilla, where there were already some hunting palaces that are cited in Alfonso X's Book of Falconry and a Hermitage called Santa María del Pobolar. This is how the first Cartuja de Castilla was built and the sixth of the Carthusian foundations in Spain.

One of the capitals of the Madrid Sierra and nerve center of the Lozoya Valley. With numerous establishments where you can buy, recharge your batteries or stay.
In addition to its urban area, the Paular monastery stands out, a must-see
